agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module for an HR software system governing employee leave accrual, requests, approvals, and year-end carry-over policies.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued leave days available to an employee, calculated as total accrued minus total approved/consumed leave."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which employees earn 1.5 leave days per month worked."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to take leave for a specified period, subject to approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (within 5 calendar days) when accompanied by a doctor's note."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-observed holidays, used to calculate the advance notice requirement."},{"term":"Carry-Over","definition":"The transfer of unused leave balance from one calendar year to the next, capped at 10 days."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The loss of unused leave days exceeding the 10-day carry-over cap at calendar year-end."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's employment contract terminates; leave requests must not extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate required as supporting documentation for retroactive medical leave requests."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per calendar month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a company policy that can be changed by HR leadership. It is not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an employee who has worked N complete months, their total accrued leave equals N × 1.5 days."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An employee's leave balance must never go below zero. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative leave balance represents consuming a resource that does not exist — this is a state integrity violation. The system would be in a corrupt state if balance goes negative.","testable_assertion":"For any employee at any point in time: leave_balance >= 0, where leave_balance = total_accrued - total_approved_consumed."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the requested leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The advance notice period is an operational policy that management could adjust without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before its start date is rejected by the system."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave can be submitted retroactively within 5 calendar days of the leave date, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The retroactive window and documentation requirement are HR policies, not data integrity constraints.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request for a past date is accepted only if (a) it is submitted within 5 calendar days and (b) a doctor's note is attached."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only the employee's manager can approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Approval authority is an organizational policy, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A leave request's status can only transition to approved or rejected by a user with the manager role for that employee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave does not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Deducting balance for rejected leave would corrupt the balance state — it represents consuming a resource for time not taken off.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request transitions to rejected status, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span past the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Leave beyond contract end date is a logical impossibility — the employment relationship does not exist. Allowing it would create orphaned obligations and corrupt payroll/benefits state.","testable_assertion":"A leave request where end_date > employee.contract_end_date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days may carry over to the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The carry-over cap is a company policy that could be changed by HR without corrupting system integrity.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, any employee whose previous year-end balance exceeded 10 days has their new balance set to 10 plus any January accrual. Forfeited days are recorded."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked 4 complete months with no leave taken","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance is 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient balance error and balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request on a Monday","when":"The requested leave start date is the following Wednesday (2 working days away)","then":"The request is rejected for insufficient advance notice (requires 3 working days)"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee submits a non-medical leave request on a Monday","when":"The requested leave start date is the following Thursday (3 working days away)","then":"The request passes the advance notice validation"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was sick last Tuesday and it is now the following Sunday (5 calendar days later)","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted and passes retroactive submission validation"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was sick last Monday and it is now the following Monday (7 calendar days later)","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because the 5-day retroactive window has elapsed"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a retroactive medical leave request within 5 days","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected for missing required documentation"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager rejects an employee's pending leave request for 3 days","when":"The rejection is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance is not reduced and the 3 days remain available"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request from March 13 to March 17","then":"The request is rejected because it spans beyond the contract end date"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carry-over process runs","then":"10 days are carried over to January 1 and 4 days are forfeited and recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carry-over process runs","then":"All 8 days are carried over to January 1 with no forfeiture"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A manager approves an employee's leave request for 2 days","when":"The approval is processed","then":"The employee's leave balance is reduced by 2 days"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request for the exact last day of their contract","expected_behavior":"Allowed — the request does not span past the contract end date, only up to it."},{"scenario":"Employee submits medical leave retroactively on exactly the 5th calendar day","expected_behavior":"Accepted — the 5-day window is inclusive. Day of leave = day 0, submission on day 5 is within window."},{"scenario":"Accrual calculation for partial months (e.g., mid-month hire or termination)","expected_behavior":"Spec does not define partial-month accrual. System should clarify whether partial months accrue pro-rata or require a full month."},{"scenario":"Leave request spanning weekends or holidays","expected_behavior":"System must define whether weekends/holidays count against leave balance or only working days are deducted."},{"scenario":"Manager attempts to approve a request that would now exceed available balance due to a previously approved overlapping request","expected_behavior":"Approval must be rejected — balance check must occur at approval time, not only at submission time, to prevent race conditions."},{"scenario":"Year-end carry-over for an employee with pending (unapproved) leave requests","expected_behavior":"Only approved and consumed leave should reduce balance for carry-over calculation. Pending requests should not affect the carry-over snapshot."},{"scenario":"Employee submits medical leave retroactively for a date before their hire date","expected_behavior":"Rejected — no employment relationship existed on the claimed leave date."},{"scenario":"Contract end date falls on a non-working day","expected_behavior":"System must determine the effective last working day for leave boundary validation."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual or carry-over logic directly impacts payroll liability. Over-accrual inflates leave provisions on the balance sheet; under-accrual creates employee disputes and potential legal exposure.","operational":"Leave management accuracy affects workforce planning, team scheduling, and manager decision-making. Bugs in balance calculation cascade into incorrect approvals and staffing gaps.","user":"Employees depend on accurate, transparent leave balances for personal planning. Errors erode trust in the HR system and generate support tickets. The retroactive medical leave policy directly impacts employee wellbeing.","risk":"Primary risks are: (1) negative balance corruption leading to unauthorized absences, (2) carry-over miscalculation creating year-over-year balance drift, (3) contract boundary violations creating legal liability for post-termination leave.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct users of leave requests; balance accuracy affects personal planning, pay, and trust in the system."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Approve/reject decisions depend on accurate balance data; incorrect data leads to poor staffing decisions."},{"group":"HR / Payroll","impact":"Leave balances feed into payroll calculations and financial reporting; errors create reconciliation burden."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Leave liability provisions on the balance sheet depend on accurate accrual and carry-over data."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Contract boundary enforcement and medical leave policies must comply with labor law requirements."}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is clear and internally consistent for the rules explicitly stated. No contradictions detected. Confidence is not higher because several operational details are unspecified (partial-month accrual, weekend handling in leave deduction, the exact definition of 'working days' including holidays). These are important implementation details but do not represent contradictions or ambiguities in the stated rules.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Accrual rate calculation for full months","Advance notice requirement for non-medical leave","Retroactive medical leave window and documentation requirement","Rejected leave does not consume balance","Contract end date boundary enforcement","Year-end carry-over cap at 10 days"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial-month accrual policy (hire/termination mid-month) — not specified","Whether leave deduction counts calendar days or working days — not specified","Company holiday calendar definition for 'working days' calculation — not specified","Whether the 5-day retroactive window for medical leave counts calendar days or working days — assumed calendar based on phrasing","Balance check timing: at submission only, at approval only, or both — not specified","Treatment of pending leave requests during year-end carry-over — not specified"],"assumptions":["The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured in calendar days, not working days, based on the spec's phrasing contrasting with '3 working days'","Leave balance is checked at both submission time and approval time to prevent race conditions","Year-end carry-over is calculated based on approved/consumed leave only, not pending requests","Accrual of 1.5 days per month applies to complete calendar months worked"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
